elements.title: Relief of Maat
elements.contents: ... that a stained-glass depiction of the Relief of Maat (pictured) has decorated Yale Law School's dining hall since 1930?

elements.title: Mary Hannay Foott
elements.contents: ... that Mary Hannay Foott's best-known poem was inspired by the true story of two men who disappeared in the Australian outback in 1877?

elements.title: Cavinti Underground River and Caves Complex
elements.contents: ... that the Cavinti Underground River and Caves Complex has a naturally formed "fountain of youth" from which visitors can drink?

elements.title: Mabel Forrest
elements.contents: ... that Mabel Forrest wrote poetry that combined the Australian bush landscape with symbols of witchcraft and Celtic mythology?

elements.title: Catullus 96
elements.contents: ... that Tennyson once quoted Catullus 96 to Thackeray while they dined?

elements.title: Ethan Sanchez
elements.contents: ... that Ethan Sanchez was nearly named "Diego Maradona Sanchez" by his father, but his mother's anesthesia wore off in time for her to dismiss the idea?

elements.title: Soviet anarchists
elements.contents: ... that Soviet anarchists believed that a dictatorship was a necessary part of a transition towards anarchy and communism?

elements.title: Aubrey Bowser
elements.contents: ... that Aubrey Bowser worked as a porter at the New York Stock Exchange before becoming a published writer?

elements.title: KonoSuba
elements.contents: ... that a KonoSuba PlayStation game features an "Underwear Judgement" system?
